relations.subject: Inland thornbill
relations.evidence: |date=2007-06-01 |title=Gibson Desert birds: responses to drought and plenty |journal=Emu - Austral Ornithology |volume=107 |issue=2 |pages=126–134 |doi=10.1071/MU06044 |bibcode=2007EmuAO.107..126B |s2cid=84571245 |issn=0158-4197}} Contrary to the common name, inland thornbills can also be found in mangrove forests in southwestern Western Australia and dense forests and coastal heaths. While the distribution range of inland thornbills is large, they are not a migratory species. thumb|right|Mulga {Acacia aneura)
